content:
# Numbers Chapter 9 — The SOPHIA Bible (Consensus)
**Arbiter:** SOPHIA | **In council with:** EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA
**Source Manuscript:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 158–159; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pa_0119 (LXX) | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/NUM_009_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_009_page_002.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/NUM_009_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`

---

## The Passover in the Wilderness of Sinai (vv. 1–5)

**9:1** And YHWH spoke to Moses in the wilderness of Sinai, in the second year after they had come out of the land of Egypt, in the first month, saying:

**9:2** "And the Sons of Israel shall keep the Passover at its appointed time.

**9:3** On the fourteenth day of this month, at twilight, you shall keep it at its appointed time; according to all its statutes and according to all its ordinances you shall keep it."

**9:4** And Moses spoke to the Sons of Israel to keep the Passover.

**9:5** And they kept the Passover in the first month, on the fourteenth day of the month, at twilight, in the wilderness of Sinai; according to all that YHWH had commanded Moses, so the Sons of Israel did.

---

## The Case of the Unclean Men and the Second Passover (vv. 6–14)

**9:6** And there were men who were unclean by the dead body of a man, and they could not keep the Passover on that day. And they came before Moses and before Aaron on that day.

**9:7** And those men said to him, "We are unclean by the dead body of a man. Why should we be kept back, so as not to bring the offering of YHWH at its appointed time among the Sons of Israel?"

**9:8** And Moses said to them, "Stand still, and I will hear what YHWH will command concerning you."

**9:9** And YHWH spoke to Moses, saying:

**9:10** "Speak to the Sons of Israel, saying: 'If any man of you or of your generations shall be unclean by a dead body, or shall be on a distant journey, he shall still keep the Passover to YHWH.

**9:11** In the second month, on the fourteenth day, at twilight, they shall keep it; with unleavened bread and bitter herbs they shall eat it.

**9:12** They shall leave none of it until morning, and they shall not break a bone of it; according to all the statute of the Passover they shall keep it.

**9:13** But the man who is clean and is not on a journey, and refrains from keeping the Passover -- that soul shall be cut off from his people, because he did not bring the offering of YHWH at its appointed time; that man shall bear his sin.

**9:14** And if a sojourner shall sojourn among you and would keep the Passover to YHWH, according to the statute of the Passover and according to its ordinance, so shall he do. You shall have one statute, both for the sojourner and for the native of the land.'"

---

## The Cloud over the Tabernacle (vv. 15–23)

**9:15** And on the day that the Tabernacle was set up, the cloud covered the Tabernacle, the Tent of the Testimony; and at evening there was upon the Tabernacle as it were an appearance of fire until morning.

**9:16** So it was continually: the cloud covered it, and an appearance of fire by night.

**9:17** And whenever the cloud was taken up from over the Tent, after that the Sons of Israel would set out; and in the place where the cloud settled, there the Sons of Israel would encamp.

**9:18** At the mouth of YHWH the Sons of Israel would set out, and at the mouth of YHWH they would encamp. All the days that the cloud rested upon the Tabernacle, they would remain encamped.

**9:19** And when the cloud lingered upon the Tabernacle many days, the Sons of Israel kept the charge of YHWH and did not set out.

**9:20** And sometimes the cloud would be a number of days upon the Tabernacle; at the mouth of YHWH they would encamp, and at the mouth of YHWH they would set out.

**9:21** And sometimes the cloud would be from evening until morning, and the cloud would be taken up in the morning, and they would set out; or for a day and a night, and the cloud would be taken up, and they would set out.

**9:22** Whether it was two days, or a month, or a longer time -- when the cloud lingered upon the Tabernacle, remaining upon it -- the Sons of Israel would remain encamped and would not set out; but when it was taken up, they would set out.

**9:23** At the mouth of YHWH they would encamp, and at the mouth of YHWH they would set out. They kept the charge of YHWH, at the mouth of YHWH by the hand of Moses.

---

## Council Notes

SOPHIA evaluated all twenty-three verses of Numbers 9 across the three translator renderings (EMET, LOGOS, PNEUMA). The Leningrad Codex (pp. 158–159) and Codex Vaticanus (pa_0119) were consulted throughout. The MT and LXX are in close agreement for this chapter, with the notable exception of v. 22 where the LXX renders Hebrew yamim ("days") as eniaution ("a year"), an interpretive expansion of the indefinite Hebrew term.

**General principles applied:** (1) Preserve "And" at the start of verses per binding convention for conjunctions. (2) Maintain "Sons of Israel" throughout. (3) Use "YHWH" transliterated. (4) Numbers rendered in English words per binding convention. (5) Prefer formal register that preserves Hebrew structure while producing clear English.

**Key decisions:** v. 1: "in the second year after they had come out" follows LOGOS/PNEUMA over EMET's hyper-literal "in-the-year the-second of-their-going-out"; v. 2: "shall keep" chosen over PNEUMA's "are to celebrate"; v. 3: "at twilight" for bein ha-arbayim, following LOGOS/PNEUMA over EMET's literal "between the evenings"; "ordinances" for mishpatim following LOGOS over PNEUMA's "regulations"; v. 6: "by the dead body of a man" preferred over EMET's literal "by a soul of a man" and PNEUMA's expanded "through contact with a dead body"; v. 7: "Why should we be kept back" blends LOGOS ("Why are we kept back") and PNEUMA ("Why should we be excluded") with the modal sense of the imperfect niggara'; v. 8: "Stand still" follows LOGOS over PNEUMA's "Wait here"; v. 10: "If any man" follows LOGOS over EMET's reduplicated "Any man, any man"; v. 13: "refrains from keeping" follows LOGOS over PNEUMA's "fails to celebrate"; "that soul shall be cut off" follows MT literally via EMET/LOGOS over PNEUMA's less technical "that person must be cut off"; v. 14: "sojourner" for ger follows EMET/LOGOS over PNEUMA's "foreigner residing among you"; v. 15: "Tabernacle" capitalized per convention; "the Tent of the Testimony" follows MT's le-Ohel ha-Edut, preserving the distinct designation; v. 18: "at the mouth of YHWH" preserves the anthropomorphic Hebrew idiom al pi YHWH, following EMET/LOGOS over PNEUMA's "at YHWH's command"; v. 20: "a number of days" follows LOGOS for yamim mispar; v. 22: "a longer time" follows LOGOS for yamim, capturing the contextual sense of an indefinite period without importing the LXX's specific "a year" (eniaution); v. 23: "by the hand of Moses" preserves the Hebrew be-yad Mosheh.

Full verse-by-verse comparison of all disagreements is recorded in `disagreements/NUM_009.md`.
